diagnosis.evidence: The selected continuous segment is valid in time, but its pelvis/root target z is near the floor instead of a standing walking height.
The current selector sorts by (length, reward_mean), so a long low-quality segment can win.
The best checkpoint has very low reward, high body error, and high non-timeout done rate.
The downstream videos share the same selected root targets and are trained/decoded from weak teacher rollouts.
Videos use MuJoCo position actuators plus root assist and should not be interpreted as paper-level control.

recommended_fix_order: Do not use the current videos as successful motion-control evidence; keep them as failed diagnostics.
Patch segment selection to require source root height/stability and prefer reward before length.
Regenerate the reference pose replay from a standing-height continuous source segment before touching VAE/diffusion videos.
Only after reference/root targets are sane, rerun teacher action-control with the same segment.
If teacher remains unstable after target selection is fixed, retrain/evaluate Stage-1 teacher before training or claiming VAE/diffusion/guidance closed-loop behavior.

continuity.selection_rule: longest segment with done_count == 0 and motion_time_steps strictly consecutive +1

camera.name: bm_pd_fixed_center
camera.robot_centering: root XY targets are recentered to zero and a fixed camera is used

summary.claim_level: MuJoCo action-to-PD visualization of per-frame local denoiser latent actions on the same verified-continuous segment

continuity_gate.selection_rule: longest segment with done_count == 0 and motion_time_steps strictly consecutive +1

continuity.selection_rule: longest segment with done_count == 0 and motion_time_steps strictly consecutive +1

limitations: This video uses one verified-continuous teacher segment and is not temporally stretched.
The controller is a MuJoCo position-actuator/root-assist diagnostic, not a paper-level unassisted humanoid controller.
The current teacher is weak, so motion quality can still be poor even though the data sequence is now continuous.
This is not a real-robot result and not official BeyondMimic Fig.5/Fig.6 evidence.

simulation.root_assist_type: external pelvis force/torque stabilizer applied before mj_step

source_metadata.denoising_method: For each true frame, repeat the current/future segment window to 21 tokens, run the local denoiser, and decode the current latent. No 21-frame sample is stretched into a longer video.

summary.claim_level: Continuous single-motion MuJoCo reference pose replay; this is the corrected replacement for the old failed discontinuous reference_action_control label
limitations: This is a continuous reference pose replay, not a policy/controller output.
It is intentionally short because the best clean teacher segment is short; the video is not stretched.
